executor_handoff.system_instructions: Implement exactly scope below. Preserve existing patterns (TerminalWriter Line/role model, SnapshotID.label, emit(.trace(...)) gating on config.verboseEvents). Do NOT widen scope: verbose-tier stdout only; no changes to JSON output, quiet/normal tiers, scheduling timing, finding/evidence rendering, or RefreshScheduler math. One path only (below). Use serena for edits. Swift 6 strict concurrency, Swift Testing.

task_brief.objective: Redesign verbose monitor stdout trace lines for readability: 1. Remove blank line between consecutive trace lines (+ before refresh-completed summary). 2. Drop the "Fetch: requesting ..." (fetchIntent) line entirely. 3. Merge the standalone cyan "<id>" context header INTO each trace line as a two-tone line: cyan "<id> →" lead + dim phrase body. Apply to EVERY trace line, not just fetch. 4. Add new TraceEvent .refreshRetry (no-change half-target backoff) rendered with a WHITE phrase (instead of dim) to highlight it. Text: "re-try scheduled in 2s".
task_brief.why: Current verbose stream = one blank line per event + a redundant cyan id header line whose id already appears in every body line. Noise. Uniform "<id> → phrase" gives a scannable cyan id-column, drops redundancy, removes blanks. refreshRetry surfaces the RFC8216 6.3.4 no-change reload backoff (already computed by RefreshScheduler.nextDelay) as a distinct, eye-catching line.
task_brief.repo_signals: - TraceEvent enum: Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/SessionConfig.swift (lines ~90-129). - TraceFormatter (enum, static format(_:)->String): .../Output/TraceFormatter.swift. Today returns full "Noun: <id> ..." lines. Becomes PHRASE-ONLY (no id, no Noun prefix). - PresentationRole + TerminalANSIStyle: .../Output/PresentationRole.swift. Styles today: bold/red/yellow/green/cyan/dim. identifier=cyan, metadata/progress=dim, success=green. - TerminalWriter (struct): Valistream/Valistream/Valistream/TerminalWriter.swift. Line struct (~18-40): {text, role, wholeLineTint, at, noTimestamp}. writeBlock (~103-121) prepends separator = state.hasWrittenBlock ? "\n" : "" between blocks (THIS is the blank line). formattedLines (~150-174): for non-wholeLineTint, emits `prefix + apply(role.style, chunk)`; prefix = timestamp(+continuation). apply (~223-233) maps style->SGR code. wrap (~176-213). - StatusRenderer (struct): Valistream/Valistream/Valistream/StatusRenderer.swift. renderTrace (~331-352): emits cyan .identifier header on context change + dim .metadata body. traceContext(of:) (~360-389): returns the id used as header (snapshotID or playlistID per case). lastTraceContext property (~41): used for header de-dup; REMOVE this mechanism. renderRefreshCompleted (~305-329): green "✓ OK Refreshed ...". writeBlock helper (~459-461). - Emit site: Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift monitorPlaylist (~27-181). refreshScheduled emitted at loop top (~51-56) using `lastChanged`. `lastChanged = changed` set at loop end (~157); `changed = media != previous`. - RefreshScheduler.nextDelay(didChange:) (Monitoring/RefreshScheduler.swift): targetDuration when changed, targetDuration/2 when not. UNCHANGED — timing already correct.
task_brief.scope: Verbose-tier trace stdout only. New refreshRetry event + its emission + formatting + white style. Out of scope: JSON/JSONL output, quiet/normal tiers, scheduling delays, findings/evidence lines, session summary, roster/playlist-info blocks (keep their existing blank separation).
task_brief.implementation_path: Two-tone line via a new optional styled lead on TerminalWriter.Line (NOT pre-baked ANSI in text, which would break wrap() width math). Phrase-only TraceFormatter + lead built in renderTrace. Blank removal via a `tight` flag on writeBlock (suppress leading separator) at trace + refresh-completed call sites only. refreshRetry chosen at the single existing emit site via the same `didChange` bool that drives the delay.
task_brief.code_level_guidance:
1. SessionConfig.swift TraceEvent — add after refreshScheduled: /// No-change reload: previous refresh returned unchanged playlist; next reload uses the /// 6.3.4 half-target-duration backoff. case refreshRetry(playlistID: String, delaySeconds: Double)
2. ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift ~51-56 — single source of truth for change-state: let didChange = refreshIndex == 0 ? true : lastChanged let delay = refreshIndex == 0 ? scheduler.initialDelay : scheduler.nextDelay(didChange: didChange) if config.verboseEvents { let delaySecs = Double(delay.components.seconds) + Double(delay.components.attoseconds) / 1e18 let event: TraceEvent = didChange ? .refreshScheduled(playlistID: presentationID, delaySeconds: delaySecs) : .refreshRetry(playlistID: presentationID, delaySeconds: delaySecs) emit(.trace(event)) }
3. TraceFormatter.format — return PHRASE ONLY (no id, no "Noun:" prefix). Map: fetchStarted -> "started (\(url.absoluteString))" fetchIntent -> "requesting" (unused; renderTrace skips it — keep harmless) fetchResult -> "fetched HTTP \(httpStatus); \(durationMs)ms; \(formatted(kb:))" validationPlaylistOK -> "validated OK" validationPlaylistFail -> "validated \(errorCount) ERROR, \(warnCount) WARN" (keep parts logic for 0-count edge: omit a zero-count part; fallback "validated findings") validationRuleOK -> "rule [\(ruleID)] OK" validationRuleFail -> "rule [\(ruleID)] finding" stored -> "stored \(archivePath)" refreshScheduled -> "next refresh in \(formatted(seconds:))" refreshRetry -> "re-try scheduled in \(formatted(seconds:))" refreshDrift -> "cadence drift \(formatted(seconds:))" continuityCompare -> "compared \u{2194} \(olderSnapshotID)" renditionAdded -> "added" renditionDropped -> "dropped" Keep private formatted(kb:) and formatted(seconds:) helpers as-is.
4. PresentationRole.swift: - TerminalANSIStyle: add `case white`. - PresentationRole: add `case notice`; ansiStyle -> .white; plainTextMeaning -> "attention".
5. TerminalWriter.swift: - apply(style:): add `case .white: "37"`. - Line: add nested `struct Segment: Sendable { let text: String; let role: PresentationRole }` and `let lead: Segment?` (init param, default nil). Keep existing fields/inits working. - formattedLines: when line.lead != nil AND colorEnabled AND !wholeLineTint: * first chunk: `firstPrefix + apply(lead.role.style, lead.text) + " " + apply(line.role.style, chunk)` * reduce wrap firstWidth by (lead.text.count + 1); continuation chunks unchanged (dim phrase, no lead). When !colorEnabled: `firstPrefix + lead.text + " " + chunk`. Leave the lead==nil path exactly as today. - writeBlock: add `tight: Bool = false`. separator = tight ? "" : (hasWrittenBlock ? "\n" : ""). Still set hasWrittenBlock = true. Thread tight through where needed.
6. StatusRenderer.swift renderTrace: - if case .fetchIntent = event { return } at top (skip the "requesting" line). - Remove lastTraceContext property + all header-on-context-change branching. - Build: let arrow = writer.mode.glyphStyle == .unicode ? "\u{2192}" : "->" let lead = traceContext(of: event).map { Line.Segment(text: "\($0) \(arrow)", role: .identifier) } let phraseRole: PresentationRole = { if case .refreshRetry = event { return .notice }; return .metadata }() writeBlock(at: at, lines: [Line(TraceFormatter.format(event), role: phraseRole, lead: lead)], tight: true) - StatusRenderer.writeBlock helper (~459): add `tight: Bool = false`, forward to writer.writeBlock. renderTrace passes tight:true. renderRefreshCompleted passes tight:true. - traceContext: add the .refreshRetry case -> return playlistID (same as refreshScheduled).
7. Tests (rewrite expected strings, no logic change to RefreshSchedulerTests): - TraceFormatterTests: update all expected phrase strings; add refreshRetry case. - NormalSessionReadabilityTests: remove blank-line expectations between trace lines; expect merged "<id> → phrase" lead lines; expect no "Fetch: requesting" line; add a refreshRetry white-phrase line if asserted. Also scan IncidentTimelineReportTests / QuietModeFindingsTests / any test asserting old "Fetch:/Compare:/Stored:/Validation:/Refresh:" strings and update.
task_brief.entry_points: TraceFormatter.swift, StatusRenderer.swift, TerminalWriter.swift, PresentationRole.swift, SessionConfig.swift (TraceEvent), ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift, then the two test files above.
task_brief.search_fallback: If filenames differ: grep verbose strings "Fetch: requesting", "next in", "Refreshed", "Compare:". TraceEvent: `grep "enum TraceEvent"`. Emit site: `grep "refreshScheduled(playlistID"`. Tests: `grep -rl "Fetch: requesting\\|validated\\|re-try scheduled" Tests`.
task_brief.validation: Build `Valistream` scheme via xcode-tools BuildProject; resolve XcodeListNavigatorIssues. Run `Valistream.xctestplan` (RunAllTests) — all pass. Manually eyeball a verbose monitor run if feasible (ExecuteSnippet rendering a few TraceEvents through StatusRenderer) to confirm two-tone + no blanks + white retry line.
